title: Reservations REST API
description: Expose a backend-only REST surface for the reservations service. No UI in this ticket — consumers are the mobile app and partner integrations. All routes require a Bearer token; unauthenticated requests return 401.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: GET /api/reservations returns a paginated list; each item carries a status of one of open|held|confirmed|cancelled.
AC2: GET /api/reservations/{id} returns a single reservation, or 404 when the id does not exist.
AC3: POST /api/reservations creates a reservation and responds 201 Created with a Location header pointing at the new resource.
AC4: PUT /api/reservations/{id} replaces a reservation; a malformed body returns 400.
AC5: DELETE /api/reservations/{id} performs a soft delete by setting status=cancelled rather than removing the row.
AC6: Any request missing or presenting an invalid Bearer token returns 401 regardless of route.

comments.author: api.owner@example.com
comments.body: Sample create payload: {"guest_id": 4821, "party_size": 4, "slot": "2026-06-01T19:30:00Z"}. Expect 201 with Location: /api/reservations/9007.
